steps.title: Identify the strongest autofiction signal
steps.content: For a novel to be “representative” autofiction that “erases the distinction between authors and characters,” it must show the protagonist's life and voice directly reflecting the author's own—typically through autobiographical patterning and a forthright, confessional narrative voice.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A demonstrates exactly this: the protagonist's upbringing is “virtually identical to Vuong's family,” the protagonist is “a young writer” (mirroring the author), and the tone is “surprisingly frank” (the confessional voice central to autofiction).

Choices B, C, and D either focus on unrelated formal elements, omit the confessional tone, or contradict autofiction's typical naming practice.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
